title: Pollo Asado

overview.description: Mexican-style marinated chicken roasted until deeply coloured, juicy and aromatic, with citrus, garlic, spices and achiote.

ingredients: 1.2kg chicken thighs and drumsticks, bone-in and skin-on
method: Make several deep cuts into the thickest parts of the chicken to help the marinade penetrate.
Mix the orange juice, lime juice, garlic, achiote paste, olive oil, cumin, oregano, smoked paprika, ground coriander, cinnamon, salt and black pepper.
Add the chicken and turn thoroughly to coat.
Cover and marinate in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.
Remove the chicken from the refrigerator approximately 30 minutes before cooking.
Arrange the chicken in a roasting tray, skin-side up.
Roast for 40–50 minutes until deeply coloured and cooked through.
Baste once or twice with the marinade and cooking juices during roasting.
Allow the chicken to rest for 10 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Achiote comes from the seeds of the annatto tree and gives many Yucatán-style dishes their distinctive deep red-orange colour.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a citrus-based marinade, use achiote for flavour and colour, and roast marinated chicken at high heat.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A vibrant Mexican chicken dish that introduces achiote and demonstrates how citrus and spices can transform simple roasted chicken.
chapman-tips: The longer the chicken marinates, the more flavourful it will be.
Make sure the chicken is well coated with the achiote paste before marinating.
Don't discard the marinade and use it as a sauce unless it has been thoroughly boiled first because it has been in contact with raw chicken.
Use a meat thermometer if you have one; the thickest part of the chicken should reach 74°C.
Resting the chicken allows the juices to redistribute before serving.
notes.batch-cooking: Cooked chicken freezes well. Allow it to cool completely, then freeze in portions. For best results, freeze the chicken with some of its cooking juices and reheat gently.
notes.leftovers: Shred leftover chicken for tacos, quesadillas, enchiladas, rice bowls or sandwiches.
